calendar.matters:
ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Nov-18-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Kelly for administrator Helen P. Tom, who was also present.

The matter was called for hearing on Status on Close of Estate.

Mr. Kelly that because the administrator prefers to sign documents in person versus digitally, they appeared in person so that the necessary documents can be signed. He stated the petition for final distribution would be signed, finalized and e-filed later today.

Based on counsel's representation, the Court took the matter of calendar.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Appear (Re: Administrator Helen Tom)
Off Calendar Oct-07-2025 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-07-2025 Off Calendar

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Oct-07-2025 Continued To Nov-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Kelly for administrator Helen P. Tom, who was also present.

The matter was called for hearing on 1) Status of Filing Petition for Final Distribution; and 2) Order to Appear/Order to Show Cause.

Mr. Kelly addressed the Order to Show Cause and explained that he did not have the hearing in his calendar for an unknown reason. The Court directed that further action was needed on behalf of the administrator in order to close the estate.

Mr. Kelly reported that the only asset of the estate was the decedent's residence, which had been in foreclosure. In order to prevent foreclosure, the administrator obtained a loan to cure the default and later sold the property. Mr. Kelly stated that he had sought assistance from an accountant to assist in reconciling the loan account and the sale and expects to complete the final accounting shortly after those issues are resolved.

Following discussion, the Court discharged the Order to Show Cause and declined to impose sanctions. The Court continued the matter to November 18, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Cindee Mayfield; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Sep-09-2025 Continued To Oct-07-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearances.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator Helen P. Tom to appear on October 7, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. An Order to Show Cause shall also be issued to attorney Mark Kelly as to why he should not be sanctioned $250 for failing to appear. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Aug-12-2025 Continued To Sep-09-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mark Kelly appeared via zoom for Helen Tom (administrator).
John Boessenecker appeared via zoom for Assignee.

Mr. Kelly confirmed he paid the sanctions yesterday, that he had an accident and broke his leg, and that he needs additional time to complete the petition.

The matter is continued to September 9, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
Status re Payment of Sanctions ordered off calendar.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Re: Payment Of Sanctions

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Re: Payment Of Sanctions

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jul-08-2025 Continued To Aug-12-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar May-27-2025 Continued To Jul-08-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Kelly for Helen P. Tom, Administrator (via Zoom).

The court inquired about the status of filing a petition for final distribution. Mr. Kelly reported that the Administrator was waiting for end-of-month bank statements as verification of balances, which are expected within the next week.

Discussion was held regarding sanctions previously issued to Mr. Kelly, with the court noting that the sanctions had not been paid by the May 19, 2025, deadline. Mr. Kelly explained that logistical issues had delayed payment, as he no longer uses office checks and was uncertain how to submit payment remotely. Mr. Kelly was informed by the clerk as to the various methods payment could be submitted. Mr. Kelly stated he would proceed with paying the sanctions through e-filing immediately following the hearing.

Following discussion, the court continued the matter to July 8, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, with the expectation that the petition will be on file before that date and that outstanding sanctions will be paid.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar May-06-2025 Continued To May-27-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (personal):
Mark Kelly, Esq., for and with Helen Tom, Administrator.

At the court's query, counsel reports regarding his failure to appear at the previous calling. Upon consideration of counsel's report, the court does not find good cause for the failure to appear and counsel is ordered to pay sanctions in the amount of $250.

Counsel then advises that an amended accounting and report is ready to be filed soon and that the petition should clear the pending issues.

The Status Hearing on Filing of Petition for Final Distribution is continued to May-27-2025 at 9:00 am in Department 204. The remaining subject matters are taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Thompson. Not Reported.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-06-2025 Off Calendar

Off Calendar May-06-2025 Off Calendar

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Apr-08-2025 Continued To May-06-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The court continued the matter to May 6, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. An Order to Appear shall be issued to attorney Mark Kelly and Administrator Helen P. Tom to appear at the continued hearing. An Order to Show Cause shall be issued to attorney Mark Kelly to show cause why he should not be sanctioned $250 under CCP Section 128.7 for failure to appear.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Mar-11-2025 Continued To Apr-08-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Kelly for Helen P. Tom, Administrator (via Zoom).

Mr. Kelly reported that the administrator experienced health-related issues but has since recovered. They are currently finalizing the accounting report and petition for final distribution and are on the verge of completing the final version. Mr. Kelly requested a 30-day continuance to allow time to complete the filing.

The court continued the matter to April 8, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-11-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Kelly for Helen Tom, Petitioner (via Zoom).

Discussion was held regarding the minutes from the October 29, 2024 hearing, where it was noted that delays in the accounting were due to medical issues experienced by the administrator. Mr. Kelly reported that the Administrator is recovering, and that progress is being made toward finalizing the accounting. However, her age and need for personal assistance have slowed the process.

After discussion, the court and counsel agreed to take the petition off calendar to allow the Administrator to file a new petition once all necessary documentation is prepared and finalized.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-29-2024 Continued To Dec-11-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Mark Kelly, Esq., for Helen Tom, Administrator.

At the court's query, counsel reports that his client has had to deal with medical issues which has caused the delay in closing the subject estate. Counsel advises that his client has recovered, and that he is now ready to clear the Examiner's notes and subsequently file a petition to close the Estate.

After additional discussion, the subject matter is continued to Dec-11-2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Lydia Villarreal. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Wong. Not Reported.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.

Off Calendar Feb-06-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

Mark Kelly, Esq., for Helen Tom, Personal Representative.

At the outset, counsel reports that an inventory and appraisal are pending in the court's e-filing queue. The court and counsel note that the next calling of this matter is set for Mar-12-2024.

The status of inventory and appraisal is taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Stephen Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Svahlin. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-23-2024 Continued To Mar-12-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Kelly appeared via zoom for Helen Tom (administrator).

Discussion was held regarding the filing of the Inventory and Appraisal, that Mr. Kelly thought the Inventory and Appraisal was to be filed at the clerk's office, and that he will e-file the document by this week. The Court instructed counsel to file either a petition for final distribution or report of status of administration by February 27, 2024. Status on close of estate is continued to March 12, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status of Inventory and Appraisal is set for February 06, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Chavez. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-28-2023 Continued To Jan-23-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Mark Kelly appeared via zoom for Helen Tom (administrator).

Mr. Kelly stated that he filed a paper copy of the Inventory and Appraisal, and that the Court advised him of efiling the document. Status on close of estate is continued to January 23, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Papers to be filed by January 09, 2024.

The Court issues sanctions in the amount of $250.00 against Mark Kelly Pursuant to CCP 177.5 payable within 10 days of the order.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Cabrera. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:
No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause will be issued to Mark Kelly (attorney) to appear on November 28, 2023, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 pursuant to CCP 128 should not be imposed against him for failure to appear. An Order to Appear will be issued to Helen Tom (petitioner) to appear on November 28, 2023, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-25-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Kelly, in court, for and with Helen Tom, in court, Petitioner.

The court and counsel discuss the pending Examiner's notes with counsel pointing to the supplemental declaration file on Oct-03-2022 and of making a personal appearance with his client for her to clear the pending Examiner's notes before the court, under penalty of perjury. The court and counsel discuss the bond amount issue and confirms that it will require a full bond. Ms. Tong is sworn-in by the courtroom clerk. Ms. Tong confirms the following under penalty of perjury: the decedent had no predeceased spouse, the decedent was never married, and the decedent passed-away with no issue.

The Petition for Letters of Administration is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

***After hearing (Oct-25-2022): the court reviews and signs the Order on the Petition for Letters of Administration.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-30-2022 Continued To Oct-25-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
